page: Amazon Web Services c7a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz, delivering 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, which provides a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the c7a.8xlarge demonstrates strong performance characteristics. Its single-core CPU performance ranks in the top tier, while multi-core performance and Geekbench scores consistently place in the top 25%. The system exhibits strong memory write bandwidth and cached read-only bandwidth, though uncached large-block read-only bandwidth falls into the average tier. The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 vector and neural network instructions, which contribute to strong performance in encryption, physics simulations, and software compilation.

This hardware profile is highly cost-efficient for compute-intensive workloads that require high CPU density relative to memory. The instance excels at processing tasks such as Redis database operations, software compilation, and light LLM inference, where it achieves top-tier token generation speeds on smaller models. However, the lack of bundled local storage and the 2.0 GB per core memory allocation represent tradeoffs for memory-heavy databases or storage-intensive applications. Appropriate workloads include batch processing, web serving, and distributed compute tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c7a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance featuring 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. It is configured with 64.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, but it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Benchmark data shows top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core capabilities, particularly in software compilation, encryption, and small-model LLM inference. This resource profile is highly efficient for compute-heavy workloads, though the memory-to-core ratio and lack of local storage present tradeoffs for memory-intensive applications. It is well-suited for batch processing, web serving, and distributed computing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 32 AMD EPYC cores, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and 12 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier single-core CPU performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.8xlarge features 32 AMD EPYC cores and 64 GB DDR5 RAM, optimized for compute-heavy workloads and software compilation.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC compute-optimized instance featuring high-speed DDR5 memory and top-tier single-core processing performance for intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
64.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 5 GHz
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core benchmarks
Baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps on the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for software compilation, batch processing, and web serving
